text: Code reviews are a crucial part of the software development process. During a code review, team members examine each other's code before it is added to the main project. This helps catch bugs early, when they are easier and cheaper to fix. Beyond finding errors, code reviews also encourage knowledge sharing. Developers learn from each other's techniques and can give helpful suggestions. Over time, this practice improves the overall quality of the team's work, as everyone becomes more familiar with the codebase and best practices. Ultimately, regular code reviews lead to better software and stronger teams.
